database_metadata.name: NCAA
database_metadata.description: A relationalized version of the Kaggle March Machine Learning Mania 2015 dataset for predicting the 2015 NCAA men's basketball tournament. The source data include season metadata, team identifiers, tournament seeds and bracket slots, historical regular-season and tournament game results in compact and detailed forms, plus 2015-specific tournament seed/slot and regular-season result files for prediction and submission.

target_qa_generation_framework.Description: Directly search for entities or records
target_qa_generation_framework.Example: Return the names of all users who have purchased the iPhone 13.
Which city had the highest average order value over the past 6 months?

target_qa_generation_framework.Description: Multi-table joins, multi-hop logic, existence/exclusion relationships
target_qa_generation_framework.Example: Which users have placed orders but never left any product reviews on the platform?

target_qa_generation_framework.Description: Inferring future behavior or state based on historical data
target_qa_generation_framework.Example: Based on historical behavior, which users are unlikely to place another order within the next 30 days?

tables.description: Game-level regular-season results table in compact form. Each row represents one completed regular-season NCAA men's basketball game, recording the season, the day number within the season, the winning team and score, the losing team and score, the winner's game location, and the number of overtime periods.

columns.name: season
columns.comment: Season identifier for the game.

columns.name: daynum
columns.comment: Day number within the season on which the game was played.

columns.name: wteam
columns.comment: Team ID of the winning team.

columns.name: wscore
columns.comment: Points scored by the winning team.

columns.name: lteam
columns.comment: Team ID of the losing team.

columns.name: lscore
columns.comment: Points scored by the losing team.

columns.name: wloc
columns.comment: Location of the winning team for the game: `H` = home, `A` = away, `N` = neutral site.

columns.name: numot
columns.comment: Number of overtime periods in the game.

tables.description: Game-level regular-season results table in detailed form. Each row represents one completed regular-season NCAA men's basketball game, recording the season, the day number within the season, the winning and losing teams and scores, the winner's game location, the number of overtime periods, and box-score statistics for both teams.

columns.name: season
columns.comment: Season identifier for the game.

columns.name: daynum
columns.comment: Day number within the season on which the game was played. This integer ranges from 0 to 132 and represents the offset in days from that season's `dayzero` date.

columns.name: wteam
columns.comment: Team ID of the winning team.

columns.name: wscore
columns.comment: Points scored by the winning team.

columns.name: lteam
columns.comment: Team ID of the losing team.

columns.name: lscore
columns.comment: Points scored by the losing team.

columns.name: wloc
columns.comment: Location of the winning team for the game: `H` = home, `A` = away, `N` = neutral site.

columns.name: numot
columns.comment: Number of overtime periods in the game.

columns.name: wfgm
columns.comment: Field goals made by the winning team.

columns.name: wfga
columns.comment: Field goals attempted by the winning team.

columns.name: wfgm3
columns.comment: Three-point field goals made by the winning team.

columns.name: wfga3
columns.comment: Three-point field goals attempted by the winning team.

columns.name: wftm
columns.comment: Free throws made by the winning team.

columns.name: wfta
columns.comment: Free throws attempted by the winning team.

columns.name: wor
columns.comment: Offensive rebounds by the winning team.

columns.name: wdr
columns.comment: Defensive rebounds by the winning team.

columns.name: wast
columns.comment: Assists by the winning team.

columns.name: wto
columns.comment: Turnovers by the winning team.

columns.name: wstl
columns.comment: Steals by the winning team.

columns.name: wblk
columns.comment: Blocks by the winning team.

columns.name: wpf
columns.comment: Personal fouls committed by the winning team.

columns.name: lfgm
columns.comment: Field goals made by the losing team.

columns.name: lfga
columns.comment: Field goals attempted by the losing team.

columns.name: lfgm3
columns.comment: Three-point field goals made by the losing team.

columns.name: lfga3
columns.comment: Three-point field goals attempted by the losing team.

columns.name: lftm
columns.comment: Free throws made by the losing team.

columns.name: lfta
columns.comment: Free throws attempted by the losing team.

columns.name: lor
columns.comment: Offensive rebounds by the losing team.

columns.name: ldr
columns.comment: Defensive rebounds by the losing team.

columns.name: last
columns.comment: Assists by the losing team.

columns.name: lto
columns.comment: Turnovers by the losing team.

columns.name: lstl
columns.comment: Steals by the losing team.

columns.name: lblk
columns.comment: Blocks by the losing team.

columns.name: lpf
columns.comment: Personal fouls committed by the losing team.

tables.description: Season metadata table for the Kaggle March Machine Learning Mania 2015 dataset. Each row represents one NCAA tournament season and records the season identifier, the season's `dayzero` reference date, and the names of the four tournament regions standardized as Region W, Region X, Region Y, and Region Z.

columns.name: season
columns.comment: Primary key of the seasons table; the season identifier.

columns.name: dayzero
columns.comment: Reference start date for the season. Game `daynum` values are measured as day offsets from this date.

columns.name: regionw
columns.comment: Name of tournament Region W for the season.

columns.name: regionx
columns.comment: Name of tournament Region X for the season. In the Kaggle convention, the region paired with Region W in the national semifinals is assigned to Region X.

columns.name: regiony
columns.comment: Name of tournament Region Y for the season. Of the two remaining regions, the one whose name comes first alphabetically is assigned to Region Y.

columns.name: regionz
columns.comment: Name of tournament Region Z for the season. Of the two remaining regions, the one not assigned to Region Y is assigned to Region Z.

data_profiling.note: Small lookup table: full enumeration provided for LLM semantic understanding.

tables.description: Prediction target table for the Kaggle March Machine Learning Mania 2015 competition. Each row represents one hypothetical 2015 tournament matchup between two teams, identified by season and team IDs, together with the predicted probability that `team_id1` beats `team_id2` and optional binary win indicators for either team.

columns.name: id
columns.comment: Primary key of the target table. In the Kaggle submission format, this identifier is typically constructed from the season and the two team IDs for a matchup.

columns.name: season
columns.comment: Season identifier for the matchup, corresponding to the tournament season being predicted.

columns.name: team_id1
columns.comment: Team ID of the first team in the matchup.

columns.name: team_id2
columns.comment: Team ID of the second team in the matchup.

columns.name: pred
columns.comment: Predicted probability that `team_id1` defeats `team_id2` in the matchup.

columns.name: team_id1_wins
columns.comment: Binary indicator showing that `team_id1` wins the matchup.

columns.name: team_id2_wins
columns.comment: Binary indicator showing that `team_id2` wins the matchup.

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (268 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Team lookup table for the Kaggle March Machine Learning Mania 2015 dataset. Each row represents one NCAA men's basketball team and maps a unique team identifier to its team name.

columns.name: team_id
columns.comment: Primary key of the teams table; unique identifier for a team.

columns.name: team_name
columns.comment: Human-readable team name corresponding to the team ID.

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (364 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Game-level NCAA tournament results table in compact form. Each row represents one completed tournament game and records the season, the day number within the season, the winning team and score, the losing team and score, the winner's game location, and the number of overtime periods.

columns.name: season
columns.comment: Season identifier for the tournament game.

columns.name: daynum
columns.comment: Day number within the season on which the tournament game was played. This is a season-relative day index rather than a calendar day-of-month.

columns.name: wteam
columns.comment: Team ID of the winning team.

columns.name: wscore
columns.comment: Points scored by the winning team.

columns.name: lteam
columns.comment: Team ID of the losing team.

columns.name: lscore
columns.comment: Points scored by the losing team.

columns.name: wloc
columns.comment: Location of the winning team for the game: `H` = home, `A` = away, `N` = neutral site.

columns.name: numot
columns.comment: Number of overtime periods in the game.

tables.description: Tournament seed assignment table for the Kaggle March Machine Learning Mania 2015 dataset. Each row represents one team's seed in a given NCAA tournament season.

columns.name: season
columns.comment: Season identifier for the tournament seed assignment.

columns.name: seed
columns.comment: Tournament seed string assigned to the team for that season. The value encodes the region and seed number.

columns.name: team
columns.comment: Team ID of the seeded team.

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (1946 rows): 10 random samples provided.

tables.description: Tournament bracket structure table for the Kaggle March Machine Learning Mania 2015 dataset. Each row represents one tournament slot in a given season and specifies which seed or prior slot winner is placed into the stronger side and weaker side of that bracket slot.

columns.name: season
columns.comment: Season identifier for the tournament bracket slot.

columns.name: slot
columns.comment: Bracket slot identifier for the game position in the tournament.

columns.name: strongseed
columns.comment: Seed or prior slot winner assigned to the stronger side of the slot.

columns.name: weakseed
columns.comment: Seed or prior slot winner assigned to the weaker side of the slot.

data_profiling.note: Large lookup table (1916 rows): 10 random samples provided.
